title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Contract Lens: Work Queue Vs Pubsub
summary: A request path adds a broker without a latency or failure reason, hides synchronous coupling behind RPC messages, and uses one global queue for work distribution, broadcasting, and replay. Planned artifact: Add and verify the work queue vs pubsub increment in pattern selection and coupling decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Work Queue Vs Pubsub · Theory Work Queue Vs Pubsub
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8780 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
